title: Teucrium puberulum
taxonomy: Teucrium puberulum is classified in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other plants in this family, such as its square stem and opposite leaves. The Lamiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are often found in temperate and subtropical regions. Teucrium puberulum is a member of the genus Teucrium, which consists of over 300 species of shrubs and small plants. The classification of Teucrium puberulum is also supported by its morphological and molecular characteristics.

It is often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves and stems are typically harvested and used to prepare teas or infusions, which are believed to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Teucrium puberulum, also known as the pubescent germander, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Teucrium puberulum include flavonoids, diterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to address digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
